inclusion_criteria: Original human studies that compare bisphosphonate users (ever-use; any agent/route) with non-users (including placebo or 0–1 prescriptions) and assess risk of cancer (all-cause or site-specific: colorectal, breast, endometrial, liver, pancreatic) or cancer mortality
Analytic study designs: cohort, case–control (including nested case–control/case–cohort), or randomized controlled trials with a non-user/placebo arm
Population from the general adult population (≥18 years), including patients treated for osteoporosis or other non-malignant bone conditions; without the cancer outcome of interest at baseline
Reports adjusted effect estimates (RR, OR, HR) with 95% CI or provides sufficient data to calculate them
exclusion_criteria: Non-human, in vitro, ecological, cross-sectional, case reports/series, reviews, editorials, or conference abstracts without extractable data

Studies restricted to individuals with pre-existing cancer, bone metastases, or oncology-indication bisphosphonate therapy only; or where bisphosphonate exposure cannot be separated from other antiresorptives (e.g., denosumab) or combined therapies
No appropriate non-user comparator or does not report cancer incidence or mortality outcomes of interest (e.g., only precancerous lesions, benign tumors, or biomarkers)
Pediatric-only populations (<18 years) or highly specific/unrepresentative subgroups only (e.g., transplant recipients), or duplicate publications of the same dataset without unique analyses
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to select human analytic studies comparing bisphosphonate users versus non-users in general adult populations for risk of all-cause and specific cancers.

Oral bisphosphonate use and risk of postmenopausal endometrial cancer.
Risk of colorectal cancer in users of bisphosphonates: analysis of population-based electronic health records.
Oral bisphosphonates and colorectal cancer: cumulative dose and duration of use are important predictors of effect.
